response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with 15+ years of development experience and genuine developer-tooling credentials (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, MCP code generators), but the role is a Level II people-management position and the resume shows no formal people-management or organizational leadership experience. Technical leadership is implied through mentoring, workshops, and founding an open-source project, yet there is no evidence of conducting performance reviews, managing direct reports, or owning team roadmaps, which are the core responsibilities here.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience, well above the 8-year minimum
Hands-on developer-tools background: test runners (Trial), assertion libraries, CI/CD and GitLab infrastructure, Selenium training, MCP tooling
Teaching and community leadership through workshops, courses, and conference talks
concerns: No formal people-management experience, a hard requirement (2+ years) and the core of the role
No demonstrated experience setting team priorities, roadmaps, or leading organizational change
Mobile-platform exposure is limited to one iOS/Apple TV stint in 2015-2016, dated for a mobile-ecosystem focus

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and testing infrastructure depth
